summary: Finished matching the remaining sysdolphin baselib RObj code by rewriting resolveCnsOrientation and then marking sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c as Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev bot reported one new match: main/sysdolphin/baselib/robj resolveCnsOrientation improved from 98.95% to 100.00%, with matched code +1492 bytes and linked code +8012 bytes for GALE01. The PR also cleaned up symbol scopes/names for many robj-local functions and data objects in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
what_changed: Reworked src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c around resolveCnsOrientation, replacing a direct inline search loop with a new static inline helper named inlined_HSD_RObjGetByType.
resolveCnsOrientation now calls inlined_HSD_RObjGetByType(robj, 0x10000000, 4) before processing orientation constraints.
Renamed the callback parameter from arg2 to update_func, making its role clearer while preserving the callback signature void (*)(void*, int, HSD_ObjData*).

Changed local variables and stack layout in resolveCnsOrientation: examples include mtx0/mtx1 instead of sp80/sp40, sval instead of var_f1, Vec3 v reused directly, and an explicit u8 _[4] pad-like local.

Replaced manual matrix-column Vec3 construction and manual sqrtf(SQ(...)) column magnitude calculation with helper calls such as HSD_MtxColVec and HSD_MtxColMagFloat where the diff shows this matched better.

Retained the same high-level orientation update behavior: normalize matrix columns, scale by the target JObj matrix column magnitudes, walk parent JObjs when needed, concatenate matrices, then call update_func for HSD_RObj_80406E74[i] and finally for 0x37 with NULL.
Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c") became Object(Matching, "sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c").

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark several robj helper functions local instead of global, including set_dirup_matrix, resolveCnsDirUp, resolveCnsOrientation, resolveLimits, expEvaluate, expLoadDesc, and bcexpLoadDesc.

Renamed many local data/rodata/sdata/sdata2 symbols from HSD_RObj_* or lbl_/jumptable-style names to compiler-style @NNN local symbols, and adjusted scopes for arg_buf and arg_buf_size from global to local.
smart_moves: Factored the RObj type/subtype search into a static inline helper rather than leaving a bulky hand-written loop inside resolveCnsOrientation; this appears to have been useful for matching without creating a new externally visible symbol.

Used existing matrix helpers, especially HSD_MtxColVec and HSD_MtxColMagFloat, to express column extraction and magnitude behavior in a way that matched the original codegen better than manual field assignments and SQ/sqrtf expressions.
Preserved a small explicit padding local, u8 _[4], in resolveCnsOrientation, suggesting attention to stack layout rather than only semantic cleanup.
Kept local-only helper functions and anonymous constants local in symbols.txt, reducing unnecessary global symbol exposure once the translation unit matched.
Only promoted robj.c to Matching after the bot confirmed resolveCnsOrientation reached 100.00%, tying the configure.py status change to measurable matching evidence.
decomp_lessons: For matching matrix-heavy HSD code, helper functions can produce more faithful codegen than equivalent-looking manual Vec3 and sqrt expressions.
If an inlined library-like helper is needed for code shape, name it explicitly as inlined_* and keep it static inline so the match does not introduce a new symbol boundary.
Stack layout details still matter after a function is semantically close; small dummy/padding locals may be required when taking a function from near-match to exact match.
When a file becomes fully matching, update both the build matching classification and the symbol table scopes/names so the repository metadata reflects the decomp state.
Local static strings, constants, jump tables, and private helper functions should be represented as local symbols or compiler-style @NNN labels when there is no evidence they are exported API.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for small finalization PRs: here the only reported new match was resolveCnsOrientation, and that justified flipping robj.c from NonMatching to Matching.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_RObj* exported API names for actual global functions, but keep internal helpers such as resolveCnsOrientation, resolveLimits, expEvaluate, expLoadDesc, and bcexpLoadDesc as local symbols in symbols.txt.
Use inlined_HSD_RObjGetByType for a static inline helper that mimics or specializes HSD_RObjGetByType behavior without creating an external symbol.
Prefer descriptive callback names like update_func over placeholders like arg2 when the argument is used to apply object updates.
Use mtx0/mtx1 and sval for temporary matrix and scalar locals when those names help communicate matrix normalization code and maintain match shape.
Represent anonymous local constants/data in symbols.txt with @NNN local names instead of speculative HSD_RObj_address globals when the data is not exported.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced a static inline RObj traversal helper whose control flow checks the high type bit, masks with ROBJ_TYPE_MASK, and optionally matches subtype using curr->flags & 0xFFFFFFF.

Matched resolveCnsOrientation by reshaping equivalent math: extract matrix columns into Vec3, compute VECMag, guard against very small magnitudes with 1e-10F, invert magnitude, multiply by HSD_MtxColMagFloat, and write normalized/scaled columns back into matrices.
Used HSD_MtxInverseConcat, PSMTXCopy, and PSMTXConcat in the parent-JObj path while normalizing columns at each step, preserving the original hierarchy traversal structure.
Added/kept u8 _[4] in the local variable block, likely to influence stack layout; evidence is the diff only, not a reviewer explanation.
Adjusted symbols from global to local so linker/symbol metadata aligned with static/internal code and anonymous data after matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only visible feedback was the decomp.dev bot report for GALE01, which confirmed one new match: resolveCnsOrientation reached 100.00% from 98.95%.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c to see whether inlined_HSD_RObjGetByType was reused or renamed.
Search for HSD_MtxColMagFloat and HSD_MtxColVec in other matrix-heavy matches to reuse this matching tactic.
Check whether remaining HSD_RObj_* data symbols around 0x80406E74 and 0x80406F14 were later given semantic names.
Compare other baselib files flipped from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py for similar symbol-scope cleanup patterns.
